rulings.Case Title: JAMES OWEN et al VS. ASBESTOS DFDTS.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Defendant D. Zelinsky And Sons, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 16, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiffs' objections to and motion to strike defendant's supplemental papers filed May 12, 2016 (supplemental amended separate statement, supplemental declaration, and request for judicial notice) are granted. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent James Owen was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and the deposition testimony of Robert Cantley preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiffs' responses identified Robert Cantley as a person with knowledge, identified what knowledge he has regarding Mr. Owen's exposure to asbestos-containing joint compound and taping mud by defendant's employees, and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Cantley does not have such knowledge. Further, Mr. Cantley testified at his deposition that the mud at issue was asbestos and defendant failed to further address the issue of whether he had foundation to make such statements. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE JONES et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 16, 2016, line 2. DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed June 6, 2016. =(503/GLW)
